defining_genetic_criterion: FMR1 CGG repeat ≥200 (full mutation); 55-200 = premutation also flagged
defining_phenotypic_features: long face, large prominent ears, macroorchidism postpuberty, intellectual disability, autism features in ~50%
typical_age_of_recognition: toddler to early childhood
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: ~50% of FXS males meet autism criteria
intervention_contraindications: avoid stimulants paradoxical worsening common
specialist_referral_recommended: medical geneticist + developmental pediatrician + FXS specialty clinic
notes: Verkerk 1991 Cell — original FMR1 gene identification with CGG repeat. PHE-0006 directly maps.

inheritance_pattern: X-linked dominant (typically de novo; lethal in most males)
defining_genetic_criterion: MECP2 pathogenic variant (predominantly female; rare male phenotypes)
defining_phenotypic_features: female with normal early development then regression at 6-18mo, hand stereotypies, gait apraxia, autism-spectrum features, microcephaly acquired, breathing irregularities
typical_age_of_recognition: 6-30 months at regression
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: distinct from idiopathic autism but often initially diagnosed as autism before Rett features emerge
intervention_contraindications: serotonergic medications often poorly tolerated; mainstream behavioral interventions less effective than Rett-specific approaches
specialist_referral_recommended: Rett specialty clinic (e.g., Rett Syndrome Research Trust network) + neurology + medical geneticist
notes: Amir 1999 Nat Genet — original MECP2 → Rett identification. Distinct trajectory from idiopathic autism.

defining_phenotypic_features: hypopigmented macules ash-leaf spots, facial angiofibroma, cortical tubers on imaging, cardiac rhabdomyomas, infantile spasms, epilepsy ~80%, autism ~40-50%, intellectual disability ~50%
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via cardiac rhabdomyoma fetal/neonatal or skin features
intervention_contraindications: standard mTOR inhibitors (rapamycin/sirolimus) FDA-approved for TSC manifestations — DO consider
specialist_referral_recommended: TSC specialty clinic + neurology + dermatology + cardiology + medical geneticist
notes: European Chromosome 16 Tuberous Sclerosis Consortium 1993 Cell — original TSC2 gene identification. PHE-0005 mTOR-pathway syndromic phenotype.

defining_phenotypic_features: similar to TSC2 but often milder; cortical tubers, skin features, epilepsy and autism prevalence somewhat lower than TSC2
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy to early childhood
intervention_contraindications: mTOR inhibitors as for TSC2
notes: van Slegtenhorst 1997 Science — TSC1 gene identification at chromosome 9q34. Distinct allelic form of TSC; somewhat milder phenotype than TSC2.

syndrome_name: PTEN hamartoma tumor syndrome (PHTS) with autism + macrocephaly
defining_genetic_criterion: PTEN pathogenic variant (germline) + autism + macrocephaly (head circumference Z >+2)
defining_phenotypic_features: macrocephaly OFC Z>+2 often >+3, autism features, intellectual disability variable, hamartomas, lifetime cancer risk (breast, thyroid, endometrial, skin)
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via macrocephaly; cancer surveillance starting adolescence
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: substantial subset of PTEN+macrocephaly cases meet autism criteria
intervention_contraindications: standard cancer surveillance from adolescence onward — Cleveland Clinic PHTS protocol
specialist_referral_recommended: medical geneticist + oncology cancer surveillance + developmental pediatrician
notes: Butler 2005 J Med Genet — PTEN germline mutations in autism + extreme macrocephaly subset. PHE-0005 mTOR-pathway syndromic.

gene_or_locus: SHANK3 / 22q13.3 deletion or pathogenic SHANK3 variant
inheritance_pattern: typically de novo (deletion or point mutation)
defining_genetic_criterion: 22q13.3 deletion encompassing SHANK3 OR isolated pathogenic SHANK3 variant
defining_phenotypic_features: severe intellectual disability, absent or severely delayed speech, autism features ~84%, neonatal hypotonia, dysmorphic features, increased pain tolerance, sleep disturbance
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via hypotonia and developmental delay
intervention_contraindications: severe phenotype; standard autism behavioral interventions calibrated for higher-functioning autism may be poorly matched
specialist_referral_recommended: Phelan-McDermid Syndrome Foundation network + medical geneticist + developmental pediatrician
notes: Goodspeed 2020 Orphanet J Rare Dis — Phelan-McDermid Syndrome Foundation 2018 international conference findings. Distinct severe-phenotype trajectory.

gene_or_locus: UBE3A / 15q11-q13 maternal deletion or imprinting defect or paternal UPD
defining_genetic_criterion: maternal 15q11-q13 deletion OR maternal UBE3A pathogenic variant OR imprinting defect OR paternal UPD15
defining_phenotypic_features: severe intellectual disability, virtually absent speech, ataxic gait, characteristic happy demeanor with frequent laughter, microcephaly, epilepsy ~80%, sleep disturbance
typical_age_of_recognition: 6-12 months via developmental delay; characteristic features by 1-3 years
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: substantial overlap with autism criteria but distinct trajectory
intervention_contraindications: anti-epileptic regimen requires specialist guidance (some agents worsen Angelman seizures); behavioral interventions calibrated for severe ID
specialist_referral_recommended: Angelman Syndrome Foundation network + neurology + medical geneticist
notes: Williams 2006 Am J Med Genet A — updated consensus diagnostic criteria for Angelman syndrome 2005.

inheritance_pattern: typically de novo deletion or RAI1 variant
defining_genetic_criterion: 17p11.2 deletion encompassing RAI1 OR pathogenic RAI1 variant
defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability, characteristic behavioral phenotype (self-injury especially nail-yanking and head-banging, hyperactivity, sleep disturbance with inverted melatonin rhythm), distinctive facial features
typical_age_of_recognition: toddler to early childhood via behavioral phenotype
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: behavioral phenotype overlaps with autism but inverted melatonin rhythm is distinctive
intervention_contraindications: melatonin therapy (counterintuitive in Smith-Magenis given inverted rhythm) requires specialist supervision
specialist_referral_recommended: medical geneticist + sleep specialist + behavioral pediatrician
notes: Greenberg 1991 Am J Hum Genet — original molecular characterization of Smith-Magenis 17p11.2 deletion.

defining_phenotypic_features: characteristic elfin facial features, supravalvular aortic stenosis, hypercalcemia infancy, intellectual disability mild-moderate, hypersociable phenotype with strangers, anxiety, hyperacusis
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via cardiac findings or facial features
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: paradoxically autism features can co-occur despite hypersociable phenotype; ~7-15% meet autism criteria
intervention_contraindications: cardiac surveillance for SVAS; calcium monitoring; standard hypersociability does NOT mean absence of social-communication difficulties
specialist_referral_recommended: Williams Syndrome Association network + cardiology + medical geneticist
notes: Ewart 1993 Nat Genet — ELN hemizygosity in Williams syndrome 7q11.23.

gene_or_locus: 22q11.2 typically 3 Mb deletion; nested 1.5 Mb in subset
defining_genetic_criterion: 22q11.2 deletion (3 Mb typical or 1.5 Mb nested)
defining_phenotypic_features: cardiac defects (conotruncal especially TOF, IAA), palatal abnormalities, immune deficiency thymic hypoplasia, hypocalcemia, intellectual disability variable, autism features ~10-20%, schizophrenia risk ~25% lifetime
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via cardiac findings or hypocalcemia or palatal cleft
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: ~10-20% meet autism criteria; substantial schizophrenia risk in adolescence/adulthood
intervention_contraindications: live-virus vaccine caution if T-cell deficiency; calcium monitoring; psychiatric surveillance for emerging psychosis from adolescence
specialist_referral_recommended: 22q11.2 Society network + cardiology + immunology + endocrinology + psychiatry
notes: Motahari 2019 J Neurodev Disord — comprehensive review of genes deleted in 22q11.2 deletion and their neurodevelopmental contributions. Multi-system phenotype distinct from idiopathic autism.

defining_phenotypic_features: developmental delay, intellectual disability mild-moderate, autism features ~20%, obesity and macrocephaly tendency, speech-language disorder, scoliosis subset
typical_age_of_recognition: toddler via developmental delay
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: ~20% meet autism criteria; one of the strongest single CNV-autism associations
intervention_contraindications: weight monitoring (obesity tendency); standard early-intervention applies
notes: Weiss 2008 N Engl J Med — landmark paper showing 16p11.2 deletion AND duplication associated with autism. Foundational 16p11.2-autism link.

defining_phenotypic_features: variable phenotype — microcephaly (deletion) or macrocephaly (duplication), intellectual disability variable, autism features subset, congenital heart defects subset, schizophrenia risk in adulthood
typical_age_of_recognition: variable; often early childhood via developmental delay
intervention_contraindications: cardiac evaluation; psychiatric surveillance
notes: Mefford 2008 N Engl J Med — recurrent 1q21.1 rearrangements with variable pediatric phenotypes including autism. Reduced-penetrance CNV.

gene_or_locus: NIPBL (most common); SMC1A, SMC3, RAD21, HDAC8 (cohesinopathy)
inheritance_pattern: autosomal dominant typically de novo (NIPBL); X-linked (SMC1A, HDAC8)
defining_genetic_criterion: pathogenic variant in NIPBL, SMC1A, SMC3, RAD21, or HDAC8
defining_phenotypic_features: distinctive facial features (synophrys, long eyelashes, anteverted nares, thin upper lip), pre/postnatal growth retardation, limb anomalies (severe limb reduction in NIPBL), intellectual disability, autism features
typical_age_of_recognition: prenatal or birth via facial features and limb anomalies
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: substantial overlap with autism behavioral profile
intervention_contraindications: GERD common requires aggressive management; growth and feeding specialist
specialist_referral_recommended: CdLS Foundation + medical geneticist + GI + ortho
notes: Tonkin 2004 Nat Genet — NIPBL homolog of fungal Scc2 cohesin loader as cause of Cornelia de Lange syndrome.

defining_phenotypic_features: Coloboma, Heart defects, Atresia choanae, Retardation of growth/development, Genital hypoplasia, Ear anomalies; autism features ~50% in subset
typical_age_of_recognition: birth via coloboma, choanal atresia, cardiac anomalies
intervention_contraindications: airway management, sensory deficits (vision/hearing) require accommodation; standard autism behavioral interventions adapted for sensory issues
specialist_referral_recommended: CHARGE Syndrome Foundation + ENT + ophthalmology + cardiology
notes: Vissers 2004 Nat Genet — CHD7 chromodomain helicase as CHARGE cause.

defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability, distinctive facial features (brachycephaly, synophrys, anteverted nares), hypotonia, autism features, congenital heart defects subset, severe speech delay
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via hypotonia and developmental delay
intervention_contraindications: cardiac evaluation; standard developmental support
notes: Kleefstra 2006 Am J Hum Genet — EHMT1 loss-of-function as cause of 9q34.3 microdeletion / Kleefstra syndrome.

defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability mild-moderate, macrodontia of upper central incisors, distinctive facial features, short stature, skeletal anomalies, autism features subset
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via dental and facial features
intervention_contraindications: dental specialist due to macrodontia; orthopedic surveillance
notes: Sirmaci 2011 Am J Hum Genet — ANKRD11 mutations as cause of KBG syndrome.

gene_or_locus: ARID1B (most common); ARID1A, SMARCB1, SMARCA4, SMARCE1 (BAF complex)
defining_genetic_criterion: pathogenic variant in ARID1B or other SWI/SNF / BAF complex gene
defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability variable, hypoplastic / absent fifth fingernails or toenails, coarse facial features, feeding difficulties, growth delay, autism features
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via hypoplastic distal nails and feeding issues
intervention_contraindications: feeding specialist for early infancy
notes: Tsurusaki 2012 Nat Genet — SWI/SNF complex mutations in Coffin-Siris syndrome. ARID1B is most common.

defining_phenotypic_features: early-onset refractory epilepsy (often in first months), severe intellectual disability, hand stereotypies, Rett-like phenotype but earlier seizure onset, autism features
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via early seizures
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: severe ID overlaps with autism features but distinct trajectory
intervention_contraindications: refractory epilepsy management challenging; ketogenic diet sometimes effective
specialist_referral_recommended: International Foundation for CDKL5 Research + epilepsy specialist + medical geneticist
notes: Tao 2004 Am J Hum Genet — CDKL5/STK9 mutations as cause of X-linked infantile spasms with atypical Rett features.

defining_phenotypic_features: postnatal microcephaly, severe intellectual disability, congenital onset (no normal development period unlike classical Rett), corpus callosum agenesis subset, dyskinetic movements, epilepsy
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via microcephaly and severe delay
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: severe ID; autism features overlap but distinct trajectory
intervention_contraindications: distinct management from classical Rett; epilepsy specialist
specialist_referral_recommended: FOXG1 Research Foundation + neurology + medical geneticist
notes: Ariani 2008 Am J Hum Genet — FOXG1 mutations as cause of congenital Rett-variant. Distinct from classical Rett (no period of normal development).

defining_phenotypic_features: severe intellectual disability, distinctive facial features (midface retraction, prominent forehead), refractory seizures, multiple skeletal anomalies, hydronephrosis, neuroepithelial tumors risk, often early lethality
typical_age_of_recognition: birth via distinctive facial features
intervention_contraindications: tumor surveillance; goals-of-care discussions given short life expectancy
specialist_referral_recommended: palliative care + neurology + nephrology + medical geneticist
notes: Hoischen 2010 Nat Genet — de novo SETBP1 mutations as cause of Schinzel-Giedion syndrome.

defining_phenotypic_features: severe intellectual disability, absent speech, epilepsy, autism features, stereotypic movements, hypotonia
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via developmental delay
intervention_contraindications: epilepsy management; AAC for absent speech
notes: Le Meur 2010 J Med Genet — MEF2C haploinsufficiency from 5q14.3 microdeletion or point mutation as severe ID + autism cause.

defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability variable, autism features, epilepsy subset, movement disorders, NMDA receptor dysfunction
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via developmental delay
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: subset meet autism criteria; overlaps with PHE-0007 GABA/Cl⁻ E:I imbalance phenotype mechanistically
intervention_contraindications: NMDA receptor pharmacology may be relevant; epilepsy management
notes: Endele 2010 Nat Genet — GRIN2A and GRIN2B NMDA receptor subunit mutations as ID cause. Mechanistically relevant to E:I imbalance phenotype.

syndrome_name: SHANK3-related autism (isolated SHANK3 variant without 22q13 deletion)
defining_genetic_criterion: pathogenic SHANK3 variant (without larger 22q13 deletion = distinguishes from full Phelan-McDermid)
defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability, autism features, speech delay, motor delay, less severe than full Phelan-McDermid deletion
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via developmental delay
intervention_contraindications: standard early intervention
notes: Durand 2007 Nat Genet — SHANK3 mutations directly causing autism without full 22q13 deletion. Foundational synaptic-scaffold autism gene.

inheritance_pattern: typically de novo deletion or pathogenic variant; some inherited
defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability variable, autism features, schizophrenia risk, epilepsy subset, language delay
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: substantial overlap; NRXN1 is high-confidence SFARI gene
intervention_contraindications: psychiatric surveillance for emerging psychosis in adolescence/adulthood
notes: Kim 2008 Am J Hum Genet — NRXN1 disruption in autism. Synaptic adhesion molecule.

inheritance_pattern: autosomal recessive (homozygous founder mutation in Old Order Amish)
defining_phenotypic_features: cortical dysplasia, focal epilepsy, language regression, intellectual disability, autism features in ~70% in original Amish cohort
typical_age_of_recognition: early childhood via seizures and regression
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: ~70% in original cohort; lower in heterozygous variants
intervention_contraindications: epilepsy specialist; some surgical options for refractory cases
notes: Strauss 2006 N Engl J Med — recessive CNTNAP2 mutation in Old Order Amish cortical dysplasia-focal epilepsy syndrome with autism. Foundational autism-language gene paper.

defining_phenotypic_features: macrocephaly, autism features, intellectual disability variable, distinctive facial features, GI symptoms (constipation), sleep disturbance
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via macrocephaly and autism features
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: ~85% meet autism criteria — one of highest-penetrance autism genes
intervention_contraindications: GI evaluation for chronic constipation; sleep specialist
notes: O'Roak 2012 Science — multiplex targeted sequencing identified CHD8 as recurrently mutated autism gene. Highest-confidence SFARI autism gene category.

defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability, autism features, distinctive facial features, hypotonia, primary tooth eruption early, short stature, cardiac defects subset
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via developmental delay and facial features
intervention_contraindications: dental specialist for early eruption; cardiac evaluation
specialist_referral_recommended: ADNP Kids Research Foundation + medical geneticist + developmental pediatrician
notes: Helsmoortel 2014 Nat Genet — ADNP de novo mutations as SWI/SNF-related autism syndrome.

defining_phenotypic_features: spectrum from benign familial neonatal-infantile seizures to severe early-infantile epileptic encephalopathy with autism + intellectual disability; specific variant determines severity
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via seizures or developmental delay
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: variable; subset with severe phenotype meet autism criteria
intervention_contraindications: sodium channel blockers (carbamazepine, phenytoin) may help loss-of-function variants but worsen gain-of-function; genotype-guided AED selection critical
notes: Sanders 2012 Nature — de novo SCN2A mutations strongly associated with autism. Genotype-guided AED selection makes this clinically actionable.

defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability, epilepsy ~85%, autism features ~50%, language delay, GI issues
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via seizures or developmental delay
intervention_contraindications: epilepsy management; emerging targeted therapies in trials
specialist_referral_recommended: SYNGAP Research Fund + epilepsy specialist + medical geneticist
notes: Hamdan 2009 N Engl J Med — SYNGAP1 mutations as nonsyndromic ID cause. Excitatory synaptic GTPase activator.

defining_phenotypic_features: distinctive facial features, intellectual disability, Hirschsprung disease ~50%, microcephaly, epilepsy, congenital heart defects, autism features
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via Hirschsprung and dysmorphic features
intervention_contraindications: GI surgical management for Hirschsprung; cardiac evaluation
notes: Yamada 2001 Am J Hum Genet — ZFHX1B / ZEB2 nonsense and frameshift mutations as Mowat-Wilson cause.

defining_genetic_criterion: biallelic pathogenic PAH variants AND inadequate Phe-restricted diet adherence
defining_phenotypic_features: in untreated cases — severe intellectual disability, autism features, seizures, eczema, musty body odor, hypopigmentation; treated cases nearly normal cognition with strict diet
typical_age_of_recognition: newborn screening should catch all cases in developed-world settings
autism_prevalence_in_syndrome: substantial in untreated cases; very low in well-treated cases
intervention_contraindications: strict Phe-restricted diet for life is the intervention; aspartame contraindicated
notes: Baieli 2003 J Autism Dev Disord — autism and phenylketonuria. Untreated PKU mimics autism; primary intervention is diet. Newborn screening usually catches.

defining_genetic_criterion: biallelic pathogenic DHCR7 variants AND elevated 7-dehydrocholesterol on metabolic panel
defining_phenotypic_features: intellectual disability, distinctive facial features, syndactyly of 2-3 toes, autism features ~50-75%, growth retardation, photosensitivity, low cholesterol
typical_age_of_recognition: infancy via dysmorphic features and biochemical screening
intervention_contraindications: cholesterol supplementation is the disease-specific intervention; photoprotection
notes: Tierney 2001 Am J Med Genet — behavioral phenotype of Smith-Lemli-Opitz including autism features. DHCR7 deficiency in cholesterol biosynthesis.

defining_genetic_criterion: m.3243A>G heteroplasmy ≥10-15% in blood (higher in muscle/CSF)
defining_phenotypic_features: variable phenotype with heteroplasmy load — mitochondrial encephalomyopathy, lactic acidosis, stroke-like episodes (MELAS), diabetes, hearing loss, autism features in subset
typical_age_of_recognition: variable — childhood through adulthood
intervention_contraindications: mitochondrial cocktail (CoQ10, L-carnitine, B-vitamins, riboflavin); avoid mito-toxic drugs (valproate, statins, fluoroquinolones, halogenated anesthetics)
notes: Goto 1990 Nature — original m.3243A>G tRNA-Leu mutation in MELAS. Hannah-Poling-relevant mitochondrial-vulnerability subset.

defining_genetic_criterion: m.8993 heteroplasmy — high (>70%) → Leigh syndrome; intermediate (30-70%) → NARP
defining_phenotypic_features: NARP — neurogenic muscle weakness, ataxia, retinitis pigmentosa; Leigh — subacute necrotizing encephalomyelopathy, dystonia, brainstem involvement; both can include autism features in childhood-onset cases
typical_age_of_recognition: variable — infancy (Leigh) to adulthood (NARP)
intervention_contraindications: mitochondrial cocktail; avoid mito-toxic drugs as for MELAS
notes: Holt 1990 Am J Hum Genet — original m.8993 mtDNA mutation as NARP/Leigh cause. Heteroplasmy-dose-dependent phenotype.

defining_phenotypic_features: myoclonic epilepsy, cerebellar ataxia, ragged-red fibers on muscle biopsy, hearing loss, dementia, autism features in subset
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood to adulthood
intervention_contraindications: mitochondrial cocktail; avoid mito-toxic drugs
notes: Shoffner 1990 Cell — m.8344A>G tRNA-Lys as MERRF cause.

defining_phenotypic_features: developmental delay, intellectual disability, microcephaly, distinctive facial features (high-arched palate, prominent central incisors), retinal dystrophy, neutropenia (truncal obesity in older), joint hypermobility
typical_age_of_recognition: childhood via developmental delay and facial features
intervention_contraindications: ophthalmology surveillance for retinal dystrophy; CBC monitoring for neutropenia
notes: Kolehmainen 2004 Am J Hum Genet — large-scale genotype-phenotype delineation of Cohen syndrome.
